question_text: A book has a mass of 2 kg. Calculate its weight on Earth where g = 10 N/kg.
correct_answer: 20 N (Newtons). Using weight = mass × gravitational field strength: weight = 2 kg × 10 N/kg = 20 N
explanation: This is a direct application of the weight formula W = m × g. Simply multiply the mass (2 kg) by the gravitational field strength (10 N/kg) to get 20 N. This is the standard calculation for Year 8 students.
